title: Bitcoin Traffic Surges to Two-Year High on Rune Protocol Activity
summary: Bitcoin transactions have spiked to over 820,000 in a single day, the highest since 2024, driven by increased activity on the Rune protocol. This surge is also boosting transaction fees, benefiting miners and the network's security.
body:
Bitcoin's network activity has reached a two-year high, with daily transactions exceeding 820,000. This surge is primarily attributed to the growing popularity of the Rune protocol, which has significantly increased the number of transactions on the Bitcoin blockchain.

The Rune protocol, known for its unique approach to tokenization and decentralized finance (DeFi) on Bitcoin, has seen a massive uptick in usage. This has led to a corresponding rise in transaction fees, which are now at their highest levels since 2024. Higher fees benefit miners by providing more incentive to secure the network, enhancing overall security and stability.

For everyday users, this surge means that Bitcoin transactions may become slightly more expensive in the short term. However, it also indicates a growing ecosystem around Bitcoin, potentially leading to more innovation and use cases. The increased activity could attract more developers and investors to the Bitcoin network, fostering further growth.

What to watch: Keep an eye on the sustained activity of the Rune protocol and how it continues to impact Bitcoin's transaction volume and fee market. This could signal a long-term shift in how Bitcoin is used beyond simple transactions.
twitterThread: Bitcoin transactions hit 820,000+ in a day, the highest since 2024. The Rune protocol is driving this surge, pushing fees up too.
The Rune protocol, a DeFi-focused layer on Bitcoin, is behind the spike. Higher fees mean more rewards for miners, boosting network security.

If you're using Bitcoin, expect slightly higher fees for now. But this could mean more innovation ahead. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-24-bitcoin-traffic-surges-to-two-year-high-on-rune-protocol-activity
standaloneTweet: Bitcoin transactions just hit 820,000+ in a day, the highest since 2024. Thanks to the Rune protocol, fees are up, but so is network activity. What's next for BTC?
